editorial.headline: Crow-Armstrong's Walk-Off Caps Dramatic Chicago Comeback
editorial.subheadline: ATH 6, CHC 7. Chicago Cubs completes the comeback.
editorial.lead_angle:
Pete Crow-Armstrong sent a screaming grounder past the drawn-in infield, chasing home Michael Busch from third base to cap a dramatic ninth-inning rally for the Chicago Cubs. The Cubs stunned the Oakland Athletics by a 7-6 count at Wrigley Field, snatching victory from Joel Kuhnel and the Athletics in front of a roaring home crowd.

The game's complexion twisted sharply in the final frame. Oakland carried a 5-3 lead into the ninth, adding an insurance run off Joel Kuhnel on a Tyler Soderstrom solo home run, extending their advantage to 6-3. But the bottom of the ninth saw the North Siders erupt. Ian Happ blasted a two-run homer to right field, narrowing the deficit to a single run. After a walk and a single, Pete Crow-Armstrong stepped to the plate with the bases loaded.

Crow-Armstrong’s clutch single, his second hit of the afternoon, secured the Cubs’ comeback. He finished with two runs batted in, joining Ian Happ’s three RBI as the offensive engines. Winning pitcher Ryan Rolison collected the victory in relief, while Joel Kuhnel wore the loss for the Athletics, unable to protect the late lead. This hard-fought win provides a vital boost for the Chicago Cubs' standing in the early summer race.
editorial.reason: Walk-off comeback from 3 runs — drama stacked on drama.
